title: Lodging at the State's House (Doctoral Project)
partners: Based on 18-months ethnography in three social housing, my thesis traces how residents’ contingent entanglements with the state produce conflicting formations of rights, care, agency, and community, revealing forms of agency that both align with and unsettle existing structures while privileging ethical and affective claimsover formal rights-based politics, with a persistent yearning for a paternalistic state care.

title: Climate Change from Young People Standpoint
partners: Despite widely acknowledged as experiencing disproportionate impacts of climate change, we know very little about how marginalized young people understand and experience environmental injustice. This project traces how young people from three urban marginalised and informal communities perceive and narrate climate and environmental harms in their everyday life.
